title: Manipur is a highly weaponised society: Former Assam Rifles DG
description: Manipur is once again on edge. After a period of relative calm — largely during the year of President’s Rule — tensions and violence have resurfaced following the restoration of an elected government on February 4. At least 11 people have been killed since April 7. The deaths have been reported from Bishnupur district, a Meitei-dominated area, and Ukhrul, where a fresh conflict has emerged between Tangkhul Naga and Kuki communities. In an interview with The Hindu, Lieutenant General Pradeep Chandran Nair (Retd) — former Director General of the Assam Rifles discusses the resurgence of violence, the evolving security situation, and the emergence of new pockets of conflict in Manipur

title: Super El-Nino, drought & heat hell: India staring at longest, hottest summer yet
description: India is experiencing extreme heatwaves, with temperatures soaring and heatwaves arriving earlier and with greater intensity. This is exacerbated by the El Niño phenomenon, which is expected to strengthen. The heat poses significant health risks and economic challenges, impacting daily life and livelihoods across the nation. Adaptation and mitigation strategies are crucial for navigating this growing crisis.

Vaibhav Gupta described his meeting at Aditya Dhar's home as a revelation, sharing that the director plays the sitar and offered specific, technically informed guidance on vocal modulation throughout the recording process. Since the film's release on March 19, 2026, music directors across the industry have sent Vaibhav Gupta personal messages saying they "haven't heard a voice like his in a long time." Previously seen on Indian Idol, Vaibhav credits the 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' experience as transformative both professionally and personally.

description: ‘Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge’ is rewriting the record books as it nears the end of its seventh week. Having already become the first Hindi film to join the Rs. 1,000 crore net club in India, the film’s total domestic net now stands at a staggering Rs. 1,135.25 crore. On Day 44 of its theatrical run, live tracking shows the film maintaining a steady grip on the audience despite a reduced screen count. With a current worldwide gross of Rs. 1,783.90 crore, the Ranveer Singh-starrer is just steps away from overtaking the lifetime global collections of Baahubali 2.

'No strategic oil reserves like India': Pakistan admits vulnerability as fuel crisis deepens
description: Pakistan admits its vulnerability to global oil shocks, lacking strategic reserves like India, which holds 60-70 days of stock. Pakistan's IMF commitments restrict its ability to offer consumer relief amid soaring crude prices.

Survivors allege life jackets were distributed only after the boat began sinking, raising questions about safety protocols.

Will not contest a Pawar family member again, says Baramati MP Supriya Sule
description: Baramati MP Supriya Sule announced she will not contest elections against any Pawar family member in the future, opting for a different constituency if necessary. Her sister-in-law, Sunetra Pawar, welcomed the decision, emphasizing the importance of separating politics and family relations. This comes after two instances of Pawar family members contesting against each other.

title: Can eating soybean cause breast cancer? Here's what experts say
description: Concerns about soy and breast cancer are largely unfounded, according to modern research.  Isoflavones in soy act differently than human estrogen, potentially reducing risk. Studies, especially from Asian countries, show no increased risk and even a possible decrease in cancer incidence and recurrence with moderate whole soy food consumption.
